title: Uruguayan Toor Dal
description: A tangy and flaky toor dal recipe featuring a Uruguayan twist. This dish is perfect for a romantic dinner with a dash of spice and a vessel that adds to the charm of the meal.
instructions: Heat oil in a kadhai over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Saute until the onion is translucent.
Add the cumin, cayenne pepper, and turmeric. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the toor dal and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the ferment-jang and salt. Mix well and continue to simmer for another 5 minutes.
Check the seasoning and adjust as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Garnish with a dash of cayenne pepper for added flavor.
Serve with a side of rice or roti for a complete meal.
Let the dal rest for 5 minutes before serving for a more tender texture.
